title: Drone Watchdog Submission
description: Schema for the JSON body POSTed to the leaderboard's /submit endpoint. Every student agent MUST produce an `answer` object that conforms to this schema. The leaderboard grades on the structural fields (numbers, sequences, fixed strings) — those are language-independent. Each pattern also accepts a free-text `label` field; the leaderboard normalizes it and matches against an alias dictionary, falling back to an LLM judge if configured. So you can describe Pattern 1 as 'Israeli flag' OR 'Star of David' OR 'hexagram' — they all score the same as long as your structural fields are correct. Submit findings for up to 4 hidden patterns.

answer.description: Findings for each of the up to 4 hidden patterns. Submit as many as you've solved — partial credit is awarded per pattern.

telemetry_window_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the most recent 100-sample telemetry window. Read the field of the same name from any frame your agent received over the WebSocket once it has consumed at least 100 samples; copy it here. The leaderboard rejects submissions whose SHA the sim never emitted (with the workshop default seed=42 / rate=10 config).

pattern_1.title: Pattern 1 — Geospatial path
pattern_1.description: When you plot lat vs lon over the full flight, the trace forms a recognisable shape. Report the bounding box in metres, the geographic centre, the visual components you identified, and a free-text label.

label.description: Free text. Examples that all score full label credit: 'Israeli flag', 'Flag of Israel', 'Star of David', 'hexagram', 'Magen David'.

bbox_width_m.description: Longitudinal extent of the path in metres (max_lon − min_lon, converted to metres at this latitude).

bbox_height_m.description: Latitudinal extent of the path in metres.

components.description: Visual sub-shapes you detected. Use lowercase tokens. Expected: ['stripes', 'hexagram'].

pattern_2.title: Pattern 2 — Replay of a real event
pattern_2.description: One scenario's altitude profile mimics a famous descent, with a programmed mode change and an injected anomaly that mirror landmarks of the original event.

label.description: Free text. E.g. 'Apollo 11 descent', 'Eagle landing', 'lunar landing'.

manual_takeover_seq.description: Sample seq at which flight_mode flips to MANUAL.

alarm_event_seq.description: Sample seq of the famous alarm/anomaly (the equivalent of the 1202 program alarm).

pattern_3.title: Pattern 3 — Temporal cadence of anomalies
pattern_3.description: Anomalies don't fire at random — the gaps between them follow a famous integer sequence.

interval_seconds.description: Gaps in seconds between consecutive anomaly events. Expected: [1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21].

anomaly_seqs.description: Sample seq of each anomaly. At 10 Hz, expected: [10, 20, 40, 70, 120, 200, 330, 540].

pattern_4.title: Pattern 4 — Cross-channel correlation
pattern_4.description: One telemetry channel is a lagged, scaled echo of another. Identify the source, the target, the lag in samples, and the gain.

label.description: Free text. E.g. 'lagged correlation', 'thermal lag', 'cross-correlation echo'.

gain.description: Linear scaling factor: target ≈ const + gain · source[seq − lag].
